scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text:
B

The new flow removed 4 steps including the 'first milestone' (the activation metric trigger), so the meaning of "completion" changed. The old flow counted up to activation at the last milestone, while the new flow completes a shorter path with a different activation definition, making the 60% → 85% comparison apples-to-oranges.

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text:
B

Root cause: The spec is ambiguous — "subscription start date" could mean either the original signup date (Jan 15) or the current billing period start (Feb 1). Engineering implemented the billing period start, but Acme expects the signup date, making this a spec ambiguity rather than a backend bug. This aligns with the escalation note that the fix is a one-line UI change (or show both dates) and that the data itself is fine.

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text:
C

Reason: Baseline combined peak is 95 req/sec (60 from Billing + 35 from API v2), which is 95% of the 100 req/sec limit. During typical spikes of 1.5–2x, the load would be 143–190 req/sec, exceeding the limit and causing 429 errors. This is documented in memory/api-usage.md.
